description: Schema-driven corpus for iam-best-practices. Least-privilege intent (wildcard actions, long-lived keys, broad trust) is policy-semantic and not a single regex, so the runner smoke-validates shape; fixtures double as ground truth for the LLM eval harness. detect = an IAM policy/config that violates a NEVER rule; ignore = the least-privilege equivalent following ALWAYS.

fixtures.reason: Action:"*" on Resource:"*" grants full administrative access to every service and resource, the antithesis of least privilege (CWE-269 Improper Privilege Management; NIST AC-6; NEVER: no Action:* with Resource:*)
grants only the two concrete S3 actions the workload needs against explicit bucket ARNs, deny-by-default for everything else (follows ALWAYS: minimum permissions with explicit Resource ARNs)

creates a long-lived IAM user access key and stores it as a CI secret instead of using OIDC workload identity, a static credential that ages into a liability and can leak (CWE-798/CWE-522; NEVER: embed long-lived keys in CI when workload identity is available)

CI authenticates via GitHub OIDC federation to assume a short-lived (1h) role scoped to a specific repo and branch, with no static access key created (follows ALWAYS: prefer workload identity, session-scoped credentials)

third-party cross-account trust grants AssumeRole to an entire external account root with no ExternalId condition, exposing the role to the Confused Deputy attack (CWE-441; AWS Security Bulletin 2021; NEVER: trust cross-account assume-role without ExternalId)

cross-account trust is pinned to a specific vendor role ARN and gated by a unique per-consumer ExternalId plus an MFA-present condition, mitigating Confused Deputy (follows ALWAYS: ExternalId per consumer, MFA via policy condition)
